channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Calgary exceeds daily water threshold as feeder main undergoes repairs
item.description: Calgary and surrounding communities used 504 million litres of water on Sunday, surpassing the city's daily threshold of 500 million litres for the first time since water restrictions were implemented...

item.title: State House intervenes in Nairobi Hospital governance dispute amid board tensions and doctors’ petition
item.description: The article reports that the Office of the President has stepped into the ongoing leadership crisis at Nairobi Hospital after senior doctors petitioned for intervention. State House has defended Presi...

item.title: Christina Frasco appointed Presidential Adviser for Sustainable and Resilient Communities
item.description: Christina Frasco, the former Secretary of the Department of Tourism (DOT), has expressed her gratitude for being appointed as Presidential Adviser for Sustainable and Resilient Communities by Presiden...

item.title: Burglary at Motherwell Clinic Disrupts Services and Emergency Care
item.description: In the early hours of Friday, criminals broke into the Motherwell Community Health Centre in Gqeberha, causing significant disruption to medical services. The burglars stole computers and other equipm...

item.title: Senate confirms purchase of vehicle for Chairperson Gilani from last year’s budget savings
item.description: The Senate Secretariat has issued a rebuttal regarding reports that a luxury vehicle was purchased for Senate Chairperson Yousaf Raza Gilani using the Senate’s budget. The rebuttal essentially confirm...

item.title: Former Tesla Promoters Turn Away from Musk and the Company’s Broken Promises
item.description: Miles Klee’s article details a growing divide among Tesla enthusiasts and influencers who have begun to distance themselves from Elon Musk and the company’s community culture. Once united by admiratio...

item.title: Cargo dhow sinks in Nungwi killing two, three still missing
item.description: In Nungwi, Zanzibar, a cargo dhow named MV Talawanda sank on Saturday, March 14, 2026, during a trip from Pangani Port to Mkokotoni Port. Two people have been confirmed dead, while three others are st...

item.title: Peter Bradshaw’s Detailed Account of the 2026 Oscars Ceremony
item.description: The 2026 Oscars, held at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles, were a surreal and politically tense event. With heightened security due to a potential drone threat from Iran following President Trump’s un...

item.title: 2002 Movie 'Men With Brooms' May See Renewed Interest Amid Curling Popularity
item.description: Ever since the 2026 Milano Cortina Games dey run, people don suddenly begin dey craze about curling, a sport wey many no dey follow before. The excitement even include drama like Canada supposed dey c...

item.title: Philippine government rice program sees longer lines amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Amid rising global uncertainty caused by the ongoing Middle East conflict, Filipinos have been lining up in long queues to purchase government-subsidized rice priced at P20 per kilo. National Food Aut...

item.title: Ongoing Tensions in the Middle East Affect Dubai Amid US-Israel-Iran Conflict
item.description: As of March 17, 2026, tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate amid the ongoing conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran. In Dubai, the situation worsened after a drone attack ta...

item.title: Capturing the Beauty of Food in Mid-Flight Photography
item.description: Photographer Piotr Gregorczyk explores the fleeting moment between ingredients and a completed meal, capturing food mid-air with astonishing clarity. Using a fast shutter technique, his work freezes t...

item.title: Ontario Premier Proposes High-Speed Rail Development Along Hwy. 401
item.description: Ontario Premier has expressed a strong interest in establishing a high-speed rail line along the busy Highway 401 corridor, a proposal aimed at improving transportation and reducing traffic congestion...

item.title: Eight BJD MLAs Cross-Vote for BJP-Backed Candidate in Odisha Rajya Sabha Polls
item.description: In the recent Rajya Sabha elections held in Odisha, eight MLAs of the Biju Janata Dal (BJD) reportedly cross-voted in favour of the BJP-supported independent candidate, Dilip Ray. The elections were c...

item.title: Astronomers Discover Sulfur-Rich Molten Planet with Extreme Surface Temperatures
item.description: Astronomers have identified an extraordinary exoplanet, L 98-59 d, located about 34 light-years away in the constellation Volans. This planet, which is more than 60% larger than Earth but only about 4...

item.title: Australian Shares Gain on Falling Oil Prices Ahead of RBA Rate Decision
item.description: Australian shares rose on Tuesday, buoyed by easing oil prices and optimism in global markets ahead of the Reserve Bank of Australia’s (RBA) interest rate announcement. The S&P/ASX 200 Index increased...

item.title: Westerly system to bring widespread rain across Pakistan from March 17 to 20, says PMD
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a weather advisory predicting widespread rain and thundery spells across the country between March 17 and 20, coinciding with the Eid ul Fitr ho...

item.title: NAFDAC recalls MR.7 Super 700000 capsules over undeclared drugs
item.description: The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) don issue alert to the public sey dem don recall all batches of MR.7 Super 700000 capsules. Di recall na because analysis show...

item.title: Iraq Explores Kirkuk-Turkey Oil Pipeline Amid Ongoing Strait of Hormuz Blockade
item.description: Amid the ongoing US-Israel conflict with Iran in March 2026, Iraq is seeking alternative oil export routes due to the continued blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. The federal government in Baghdad is e...

item.title: Scottish Parliament to Decide on Legalising Assisted Dying for Terminally Ill Adults
item.description: MSPs in the Scottish Parliament are set to vote on the Assisted Dying for Terminally Ill Adults (Scotland) Bill, which would permit adults aged 18 and over with mental capacity, and who are expected t...
